title: Roasted British Lamb Roast
description: A mustardy and velvety British dish featuring roasted lamb and naan bread, perfect for a brunch mood, this recipe is also shojin-friendly and inspired by Northern Irish cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 220 degrees Celsius.
In a small bowl, mix together dijon mustard, olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the mixture all over the lamb roast, making sure to coat it evenly.
Place the lamb roast in a roasting pan and put it in the oven.
Roast the lamb for 20 minutes per kilogram, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 60 degrees Celsius.
While the lamb is roasting, prepare the naan bread by wrapping it in foil and heating it in the oven for 5 minutes.
Once the lamb is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Slice the lamb into thin pieces and serve with the naan bread and rosemary sprigs.
